fields.label: Certificate File Name
fields.description: Name for and, optionally, path to the generated certificate file. /nsconfig/ssl/ is the default path.

fields.label: Certificate Format
fields.description: Format in which the certificate is stored on the appliance

fields.label: Certificate Request File Name
fields.description: Name for and, optionally, path to the certificate-signing request (CSR). /nsconfig/ssl/ is the default path.

fields.label: CA Certificate File Name
fields.description: Name of the CA certificate file that issues and signs the Intermediate-CA certificate or the end-user client and server certificates

fields.label: CA Certificate File Format
fields.description: Format of the CA certificate

fields.label: CA Private Key File Name
fields.description: Private key, associated with the CA certificate that is used to sign the Intermediate-CA certificate or the end-user client and server certificate. If the CA key file is password protected, the user is prompted to enter the pass phrase that was used to encrypt the key.

fields.label: CA Private Key File Format
fields.description: Format of the CA certificate

fields.label: Private Key File Name
fields.description: Name for and, optionally, path to the private key. You can either use an existing RSA or DSA key that you own or create a new private key on the Netscaler ADC. This file is required only when creating a self-signed Root-CA certificate. The key file is stored in the /nsconfig/ssl directory by default.

fields.label: CA Serial File Number
fields.description: Serial number file maintained for the CA certificate. This file contains the serial number of the next certificate to be issued or signed by the CA.

fields.label: Private Key Format
fields.description: Format in which the key is stored on the appliance

fields.label: Additional Fields
fields.description: (JSON object; supported keys: pempassphrase, subjectaltname, days)

fields.label: Certificate-Key Pair Name
fields.description: Name for the certificate and private-key pair

fields.label: Certificate File Name
fields.description: Name of and, optionally, path to the X509 certificate file that is used to form the certificate-key pair. /nsconfig/ssl/ is the default path.

fields.label: Private Key File Name
fields.description: Name of and, optionally, path to the X509 certificate file that is used to form the certificate-key pair. /nsconfig/ssl/ is the default path.

fields.label: Certificate Format
fields.description: Input format of the certificate and the private-key files. The three formats supported by the appliance are: PEM - Privacy Enhanced Mail DER - Distinguished Encoding Rule PFX - Personal Information Exchange.

fields.label: Password
fields.description: Input format of the certificate and the private-key files. The three formats supported by the appliance are: PEM - Privacy Enhanced Mail DER - Distinguished Encoding Rule PFX - Personal Information Exchange.

fields.label: Notify When Expires
fields.description: Whether to alert when the certificate is about to expire

fields.label: Notification Period (Days)
fields.description: Time, in number of days, before certificate expiration, at which to generate an alert that the certificate is about to expire

fields.label: Certificate Bundle
fields.description: Whether to parse the certificate chain as a single file after linking the server certificate to its issuer's certificate within the file

fields.label: File Name
fields.description: Name of the file. It should not include filepath.

fields.label: File Name
fields.description: Name of the file. It should not include filepath.

fields.label: Put Output in Field
fields.description: The name of the output field to put the binary file data in

fields.label: Input Data Field Name
fields.description: The name of the incoming field containing the binary file data to be processed

fields.label: Options
fields.description: (JSON object; supported keys: fileName)
